title: Latent tyre-state filter features with stint resets
summary: A new F1 state-space paper models tyre pace as a latent process with pit-stop resets and compound-specific degradation.
rationale: A new F1 state-space paper models tyre pace as a latent process with pit-stop resets and compound-specific degradation. That is a better match to this task than raw cumulative degradation, because the target is really about unobserved tyre state crossing a stop threshold.
implementation_hint: Fit a simple per-driver-race Kalman/state-space filter on lap times within stints using compound-specific slopes and pit resets. Export posterior latent pace, latent degradation slope, one-step-ahead residual, and post-reset freshness features into the tree model.
expected_effect: Should capture hidden wear and abnormal pace decay more cleanly than raw rolling means, especially when observed lap time is noisy from traffic or mistakes.
risk: Model misspecification can hurt if lap-time noise from race incidents dominates tyre signal; keep the latent model lightweight and use OOF-generated features only.
